info.title: Loadrite Cloud API
info.description:
Developer Resource: Loadrite Cloud API for InsightHQ                                                        
Welcome to the Developer Resource page for integrating with the Loadrite suite of API endpoints for accessing InsightHQ data.
# Key Points:
* API access to InsightHQ data requires the following items. Please contact your local Loadrite dealer for onboarding enablement & support of the following prerequisites;                 
    * A valid, active InsightHQ subscription for each Loadrite scale device generating payload transactional data.                                                                                                                                                
    * A valid, active InsightHQ user account with Manager-level permissions for the sites that those Loadrite scale devices are assigned to.                                                                          
    * A valid, active Loadrite Identity (LID) credential pairing to generate & revoke API access tokens.                                                                              
    * A valid, active API access token to use with the V2 Authorisation object.
* Our API design is organized around REST, JSON, and HTTP best practices. The primary endpoint is https://apicloud.loadrite-myinsighthq.com/api/v2/object, and the three objects cover Authorization, Contextual data, and Scale Data payload transactions.                                                                                     
Simple definitions for the data values provided in the responses are available via the Definitions button at the top of this page. Please note that not all values are always available as some depend on device firmware or operator discretion. Please also note that if there is no value to return, those nulls will be stripped from responses.
* Tokens can be generated via the Tokens button in the top right corner of this page.
API access tokens can be generated for durations of 3, 6, or 12 months. Upon expiry, tokens can be refreshed automatically or manually. If the automatic refresh option is selected, please expect a 401 error as a response to an expired token which will trigger the refresh token to automatically generate a new access token and restart the cycle.
* Support for CORS can be enabled upon request. Please consult your local Loadrite dealer and provide your domain/subdomain details during onboarding. 
* Test Data is available upon request. Please contact your local Loadrite Dealer.
* All the methods are versioned as V2 and requests are constrained by the following mechanisms to control the performance impact of requests for very large data sets;
    * A Site's Scale Data responses are compiled into a cache of pages of up to 150 records. The first records set will be served up as Page 1 and the associated meta data will indicate the sizing of response.
    * Subsequent requests can be for the subsequent or nominated pages. If a request is made within 10 minutes, the data will be served from cache and meta data will indicate this.
    * Day limits are applied to Scale Data responses - the Loading, Conveyor, Haul, and Downtime objects are limited to 31 historic days and the Positions object is limited to 7 historic days.
    * Day limits are applied to Context responses - the Schedule objects are limited to 30 historic days.
* There is also a new V3 method for requesting Processing Downtime data. The schema is the same as supplied in V2 but the data source is different. The intended users of this new V3 method are customers using the Update feature for processing downtime event classification.
Please note that the performance control mechanisms designed for requests to V2 methods, do not apply to requests made to the V3 method for requesting Processing Downtime data.

get.description:
This endpoint returns events and related payload data for the requested site.

- Requires a valid JWT Bearer token in the Authorization header.
- Supports filtering by site, time range, scale IDs, and events.
- Returns paginated results with metadata.
- If no data is found, returns 204 No Content.
- If the request is invalid or unauthorized, returns appropriate error codes.

Bearer.description: JWT Authorization header using the Bearer scheme. Enter your token in the text input below.
